findings.title: Missing Tenant Isolation — No organizationId in Query
findings.description: Prisma query without org scope. Attacker accesses other tenants' data by guessing record IDs.
findings.fix: Add where: { organizationId: session.organizationId } to all tenant-scoped queries.

findings.title: OS Command Injection
findings.description: User input in shell command. Attacker appends ; rm -rf / or reverse shell.
findings.fix: Use spawn() with array args (no shell). Never interpolate user input into commands.

findings.title: OS Command Injection
findings.description: User input in shell command. Attacker appends ; rm -rf / or reverse shell.
findings.fix: Use spawn() with array args (no shell). Never interpolate user input into commands.
findings.code: await exec(`DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW IF EXISTS ${view.name} CASCADE;`);

findings.title: Deserialization of Encoded User Input
findings.description: Decoding + parsing user data. If object has __proto__ or constructor, attacker achieves prototype pollution or code execution.
findings.fix: Validate decoded data with Zod schema before use. Strip __proto__/constructor keys.

findings.title: Auth Endpoint Without Rate Limiting
findings.description: Auth endpoint without rate limiting. Attacker brute-forces credentials or enumerates users.
findings.fix: Add dedicated auth rate limiter (e.g., 5 attempts/min per IP+email combo).

findings.title: XSS via dangerouslySetInnerHTML
findings.code: // Helpers — render inline markdown as React elements (no dangerouslySetInnerHTML)
findings.description: Raw HTML injection in React component. If any user content reaches this, it is stored XSS.
findings.fix: Use DOMPurify.sanitize() or render with React elements instead of raw HTML.

findings.title: 7 Vulnerable Dependencies Found (run npm audit for details)
findings.description: npm audit found known vulnerabilities in dependencies.
findings.fix: Run npm audit fix. Review unfixable vulnerabilities for reachability.

findings.title: DNS Rebinding — URL Validated Before Resolution
findings.description: URL validated at check time, but DNS may rebind to internal IP at fetch time. State actor uses DNS rebinding to bypass SSRF protection.
findings.fix: Resolve DNS, validate IP, then connect to the resolved IP directly (pin the resolution).

findings.title: Host Header Used in Application Logic
findings.description: Host/forwarded headers are attacker-controlled. If used for URL construction, enables cache poisoning, password reset poisoning, or SSRF.
findings.fix: Validate against allowlist of known hosts. Never use raw Host header for redirects or URL construction.

findings.title: Host Header Used in Application Logic

findings.fix: Validate against allowlist of known hosts. Never use raw Host header for redirects or URL construction.

findings.title: Weak Key Derivation — Hash Instead of KDF
findings.description: Using raw hash for key derivation instead of proper KDF. Attacker with access to ciphertext can brute-force the key faster.
findings.fix: Use scrypt, argon2id, or PBKDF2 with high iteration count for key derivation.

findings.title: Weak Key Derivation — Hash Instead of KDF

findings.fix: Use scrypt, argon2id, or PBKDF2 with high iteration count for key derivation.

findings.title: Insecure Randomness (Math.random) for Security Context
findings.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure. If used for tokens/IDs, attacker predicts next value.
findings.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.randomUUID() for security-sensitive values.

findings.title: Timing Attack — Non-Constant-Time Comparison
findings.description: String comparison on secret value leaks character-by-character via response time. Attacker recovers secret with ~256*N requests.
findings.fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(a), Buffer.from(b)).

findings.title: Timing Attack — Non-Constant-Time Comparison
findings.description: String comparison on secret value leaks character-by-character via response time. Attacker recovers secret with ~256*N requests.
findings.fix: Use crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(a), Buffer.from(b)).

findings.title: Large File in Git (>1MB): apps/web/public/graphics/demo-dashboard.webm
findings.code: Large file may contain embedded secrets or sensitive data
findings.description: Large files in git may contain embedded credentials, certificates, or sensitive data.
findings.fix: Review file contents. Use git-lfs for legitimate large files.

findings.title: Large File in Git (>1MB): tests/security/results/payloads/bomb.nessus
findings.code: Large file may contain embedded secrets or sensitive data
findings.description: Large files in git may contain embedded credentials, certificates, or sensitive data.
findings.fix: Review file contents. Use git-lfs for legitimate large files.
